babyLearning.en: growing more sure of itself, tiny fingerprints forming
babyLearning.hi: khud par aur yaqeen ke saath badh raha hai, nanhe fingerprints ban rahe hain

title.hi: Ummeed Thaame Rakhne Layak Hai

insight.en: Hope is not a foolish thing to carry. It is the quiet trust that tomorrow can be good — and your baby is already learning it from you.
insight.hi: Ummeed thaamna koi bewakoofi nahi hai. Yeh woh shaant bharosa hai ki kal achha ho sakta hai — aur aapka baby pehle se hi yeh aapse seekh raha hai.

expanded.en: As your first trimester closes, you may notice a softer feeling settling in — a careful, growing hope. It is tempting to guard against it, as if hoping too much might tempt disappointment. But hope is not naïve; it is brave. To carry a child is to bet quietly on the future, day after day, and that is its own kind of courage. Let yourself hold this hope gently, without needing to defend it. The warmth of your trust in what is coming reaches your baby too — and it is among the first gifts you will ever give.
expanded.hi: Jaise aapka pehla trimester poora hota hai, aap ek narm ehsaas basta hua mehsoos kar sakti hain — ek sambhaali hui, badhti ummeed. Iske khilaaf pehra dena sahaj hai, jaise zyada ummeed karna nirashaa ko nyota de de. Par ummeed bholi nahi hai; yeh bahaadur hai. Ek bachche ko paalna matlab chupchaap bhavishya par daanv lagana, din-ba-din, aur yahi apni ek himmat hai. Khud ko yeh ummeed narmi se thaamne dein, bina ise bachaaye. Aane wale par aapke bharose ki garmjoshi aapke baby tak bhi pohanchti hai — aur yeh un pehle tohfon mein se hai jo aap kabhi denge.

deepDive.en: Psychologists find that hopeful thinking is a learnable skill, not a fixed trait — and parents who model optimism quietly pass on more resilient ways of seeing the world. Hope is taught long before it is spoken.
deepDive.hi: Psychologists paate hain ki ummeed bhari soch ek seekhne layak kaushal hai, koi tay swabhaav nahi — aur jo parents optimism dikhaate hain woh chupchaap duniya ko dekhne ke zyada lachile tareeke aage dete hain. Ummeed bole jaane se bahut pehle sikhaayi jaati hai.

remember.en: Holding hope is its own quiet act of courage.
remember.hi: Ummeed thaamna apni ek shaant himmat ka kaam hai.

title.hi: Do Beej

summary.en: Two seeds choose differently, and one learns that growth needs a leap of faith.
summary.hi: Do beej alag chunaav karte hain, aur ek seekhta hai ki badhne ke liye bharose ki ek chhalaang chahiye.

body.en:
Two small seeds lay side by side in the dark, warm soil, waiting through the long winter. As spring drew near, the first seed stirred with longing. 'I want to send my roots deep into the earth,' it said, 'and push my little shoot up towards the sun.' And so, full of hope, it began to grow.

The second seed was afraid. 'If I send roots down, who knows what I might meet,' it worried. 'If I push upward, I might be stepped on, or the frost might bite me. It is safer to wait.' So it stayed curled tight, holding itself back.

When spring arrived in full, the first seed had become a bright green shoot, drinking the sun and dancing in the breeze. The second seed waited and waited in the dark — until a hungry hen, scratching the ground, found it and ate it up. Sometimes the safest-seeming choice is to stay closed; but only those who dare to grow ever feel the warmth of the sun.
body.hi:
Do chhote beej andheri, garm mitti mein aas-paas pade the, lambi sardi bhar intezaar karte. Jab basant paas aaya, pehla beej chaahat se hila. 'Main apni jadein dharti mein gehre bhejna chaahta hoon,' usne kaha, 'aur apna nanha ankur sooraj ki taraf upar dhakelna chaahta hoon.' Aur is tarah, ummeed se bhar kar, woh badhne laga.

Doosra beej darra hua tha. 'Agar main jadein neeche bhejun, pata nahi kya milega,' woh chinta karta. 'Agar main upar dhakelun, mujhe koi kuchal sakta hai, ya paala mujhe daseg. Intezaar karna zyada surakshit hai.' To woh kasa hua simta raha, khud ko rok kar.

Jab basant poori tarah aaya, pehla beej ek chamkila hara ankur ban chuka tha, sooraj peeta aur hawa mein jhoomta. Doosra beej andhere mein intezaar karta raha — jab tak ek bhookhi murgi, zameen kuredti hui, use dhoondh kar kha na gayi. Kabhi-kabhi sabse surakshit lagne wala chunaav band rehna hota hai; par sirf wahi jo badhne ki himmat karte hain kabhi sooraj ki garmi mehsoos karte hain.

title.en: Tell your baby about all the ways you are getting ready for them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko un saare tareekon ke baare mein bataayein jinse aap unke liye taiyaar ho rahi hain.

motivation.en: Every small preparation is a quiet way of saying 'I cannot wait for you.'
motivation.hi: Har chhoti taiyaari ek shaant tareeka hai yeh kehne ka ki 'mujhe tumhara besabri se intezaar hai.'

title.hi: Main Tumhare Liye Ummeed Rakhti Hoon

affirmation.en: Each day I carry a quiet hope for you — for a life full of warmth, safety and love.
affirmation.hi: Har din main tumhare liye ek shaant ummeed rakhti hoon — ek aisi zindagi ke liye jo garmjoshi, surakshaa aur pyaar se bhari ho.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: Hope Is Allowed To Be Quiet
title.hi: Ummeed Ko Shaant Rehne Ki Ijaazat Hai

content.en: I do not have to feel certain to feel hopeful. A small, soft hope is enough to carry me forward today.
content.hi: Ummeed mehsoos karne ke liye mujhe nishchit hone ki zaroorat nahi. Ek chhoti, narm ummeed mujhe aaj aage le jaane ke liye kaafi hai.

remember.en: Even a quiet hope is still hope.
remember.hi: Ek shaant ummeed bhi phir bhi ummeed hi hai.

babyLearning.en: tiny vocal cords beginning to form, ready for a first cry one day
babyLearning.hi: nanhe vocal cords banna shuru, ek din pehli kilkari ke liye taiyaar

title.hi: Sabse Achha Abhi Aana Baaki Hai

insight.en: However full your heart feels now, the most tender moments are still ahead. The best of this journey has not yet arrived.
insight.hi: Aapka dil abhi chaahe kitna bhi bhara mehsoos kare, sabse naazuk pal abhi aage hain. Is safar ka sabse achha abhi nahi aaya.

expanded.en: The first trimester can feel like a long, quiet wait — a season of patience, sometimes of worry, often invisible to the world around you. But everything you are doing now is the gentle beginning of something far bigger. The first flutter you will feel, the first time you see that little face, the small hand that one day wraps around your finger — these are still ahead of you. It can help, on heavy days, to remember that you are not at the peak of this story. You are at the start. The most beautiful chapters are still being written.
expanded.hi: Pehla trimester ek lambe, shaant intezaar jaisa lag sakta hai — sabra ka ek daur, kabhi chinta ka, aksar aaspaas ki duniya ko na dikhne wala. Par aap abhi jo kuch kar rahi hain woh kisi bahut bade ki naram shuruaat hai. Pehli halki harkat jo aap mehsoos karengi, pehli baar jab aap woh nanha chehra dekhengi, woh chhota haath jo ek din aapki ungli ke around lipta hoga — yeh sab abhi aage hain. Bhaari dinon par yeh yaad rakhna madad kar sakta hai ki aap is kahani ke shikhar par nahi hain. Aap shuruaat par hain. Sabse sundar adhyay abhi likhe ja rahe hain.

deepDive.en: Researchers studying anticipation find that looking forward to a positive event can bring as much joy as the event itself. The waiting is not empty time — it is part of the happiness.
deepDive.hi: Anticipation par research karne wale paate hain ki kisi achhe pal ka intezaar utni hi khushi laa sakta hai jitni woh pal khud. Intezaar khaali samay nahi hai — yeh khushi ka hissa hai.

remember.en: You are at the start of this story, not the peak.
remember.hi: Aap is kahani ki shuruaat par hain, shikhar par nahi.

title.hi: Teen Machhliyaan

summary.en: Three fish meet danger differently, and foresight saves the wisest one.
summary.hi: Teen machhliyaan khatre ka alag saamna karti hain, aur door-andeshi sabse samajhdaar ko bachaati hai.

body.en:
In a quiet pond lived three fish who were great friends. One was wise and always thought ahead, one was clever and reacted quickly, and one simply drifted along, trusting that things would sort themselves out.

One evening, the wise fish overheard two fishermen by the bank. 'This pond is full,' they said. 'We will return tomorrow with our nets.' At once the wise fish gathered the others. 'We must leave for the river tonight, while we still can.' But the clever fish said, 'Let us wait and see — I will think of something when the time comes,' and the drifting fish yawned, 'Tomorrow is far away.'

So only the wise fish swam off through the narrow stream to the safe, wide river that very night. The next day the nets came down. The clever fish, with great effort, just barely wriggled free; but the one who had drifted was caught. The wisest plans, the old fish later said, are the ones we make before the danger arrives.
body.hi:
Ek shaant talaab mein teen machhliyaan rehti thi jo gehri dost thi. Ek samajhdaar thi aur hamesha aage ki sochti, ek chalaak thi aur jaldi react karti, aur ek bas behti rehti, yeh bharosa karke ki cheezein khud sambhal jaayengi.

Ek shaam, samajhdaar machhli ne kinare par do machhuaron ki baat suni. 'Yeh talaab bhara hua hai,' unhone kaha. 'Hum kal apne jaal leke wapas aayenge.' Turant samajhdaar machhli ne baaki ko ikattha kiya. 'Humein aaj raat hi nadi ke liye nikalna chahiye, jab tak hum nikal sakte hain.' Par chalaak machhli boli, 'Ruk kar dekhte hain — samay aane par main kuch soch lungi,' aur behti machhli ne jamhaai li, 'Kal abhi door hai.'

To sirf samajhdaar machhli usi raat sankri dhaara se hokar surakshit, chaudi nadi ki taraf tair gayi. Agle din jaal gire. Chalaak machhli, bahut mehnat se, bamushkil chhoot payi; par jo behti rahi thi woh pakdi gayi. Sabse samajhdaar yojnayein, boodhi machhli ne baad mein kaha, woh hain jo hum khatra aane se pehle banaate hain.

title.en: Tell your baby what you imagine your very first meeting will feel like.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap kalpana karti hain ki aapki sabse pehli mulaaqaat kaisi mehsoos hogi.

motivation.en: Picturing that first moment makes the love that is already growing feel real.
motivation.hi: Us pehle pal ki kalpana karna us pyaar ko, jo pehle se badh raha hai, asli mehsoos karaata hai.

title.hi: Hamari Pehli Mulaaqaat Ki Kalpana

description.en: A gentle meditation picturing the day you meet
description.hi: Us din ki kalpana karta ek naram dhyan jab aap milenge

affirmation.en: One day I will hold you in my arms — and until then, I hold you in my heart.
affirmation.hi: Ek din main tumhe apni baahon mein thaamungi — aur tab tak, main tumhe apne dil mein thaame rakhti hoon.

introduction.en: Sit or lie back comfortably and close your eyes. Take three slow breaths. Now gently imagine the day you first meet your baby — the soft weight in your arms, their warmth against you, the quiet of that first hello. Let the warmth of that picture fill you.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein ya letein aur aankhein band karein. Teen dheemi saansein lein. Ab pyaar se us din ki kalpana karein jab aap pehli baar apne baby se milengi — aapki baahon mein woh naram bhaar, aapke khilaaf unki garmjoshi, us pehli mulaaqaat ki shaanti. Us tasveer ki garmjoshi ko khud mein bharne dein.

closingThought.en: That meeting is coming, a little closer each day. Carry its warmth with you now.
closingThought.hi: Woh mulaaqaat aa rahi hai, har din thodi aur paas. Uski garmjoshi ko abhi apne saath le jaayein.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Kal Ki Taraf Saans Lena

content.en: Breathe in slowly and think 'I am here now.' Breathe out and think 'good things are coming.' Repeat gently for one quiet minute.
content.hi: Dheere se saans andar lein aur sochein 'main abhi yahan hoon.' Saans baahar chhodein aur sochein 'achhi cheezein aa rahi hain.' Ek shaant minute tak pyaar se dohraayein.

remember.en: Each calm breath carries you gently toward what is coming.
remember.hi: Har shaant saans aapko us cheez ki taraf pyaar se le jaati hai jo aa rahi hai.

babyLearning.en: growing steadily now, settling into a calmer rhythm
babyLearning.hi: ab sthir tarah se badh raha hai, ek zyada shaant lay mein bas raha hai

title.hi: Banne Ka Ek Mausam

insight.en: This whole stretch of waiting is not a pause before life begins. It is a season of becoming — for your baby, and for you.
insight.hi: Yeh poora intezaar ka daur zindagi shuru hone se pehle ka viraam nahi hai. Yeh banne ka ek mausam hai — aapke baby ke liye, aur aapke liye.

expanded.en: It is easy to think of pregnancy as a waiting room — somewhere you sit until the real thing begins. But these months are not a blank space; they are a season of quiet becoming. Your baby is being formed cell by cell, and you are being formed too: in patience, in tenderness, in a love that is learning how to stretch. Nothing here is wasted. Every gentle day, every small worry weathered, every hope held close is shaping who you both are. You are not waiting for life to start. You are already deep inside it.
expanded.hi: Pregnancy ko ek waiting room ki tarah sochna aasaan hai — koi jagah jahan aap baithti hain jab tak asli cheez shuru na ho. Par yeh maheene ek khaali sthaan nahi hain; yeh shaant banne ka ek mausam hain. Aapka baby koshika dar koshika ban raha hai, aur aap bhi ban rahi hain: sabra mein, narmi mein, ek aise pyaar mein jo phailna seekh raha hai. Yahan kuch bekaar nahi jaata. Har naram din, har chhoti chinta jo paar ki, har ummeed jo paas thaami — yeh sab gadh raha hai ki aap dono kaun hain. Aap zindagi shuru hone ka intezaar nahi kar rahi. Aap pehle se hi uske andar gehre hain.

deepDive.en: Experts describe pregnancy as a developmental stage for the parent, not just the child — a period of identity growth as real and lasting as any milestone of childhood. You are growing, too.
deepDive.hi: Visheshagya pregnancy ko ek parent ke liye ek vikaas ka charan bataate hain, sirf bachche ke liye nahi — pehchaan ke badhne ka ek aisa daur jo bachpan ke kisi bhi padaav jitna hi asli aur sthayi hai. Aap bhi badh rahi hain.

remember.en: You are not waiting for life — you are already living it.
remember.hi: Aap zindagi ka intezaar nahi kar rahi — aap pehle se ise jee rahi hain.

title.hi: Ram Ka Ayodhya Lautna

summary.en: After long years away, a beloved prince returns, and a whole city lights up with joy.
summary.hi: Lambe saalon ke baad, ek priya rajkumar lautta hai, aur ek poora shehar khushi se jagmaga uthta hai.

body.en:
Long ago, the beloved prince Rama was sent far from his kingdom of Ayodhya for fourteen long years. Through forests and trials, he and his wife Sita and his loyal brother Lakshmana journeyed on, never losing their courage or their goodness, even in the hardest of days.

Back in Ayodhya, the people waited and hoped. They never forgot Rama, and they trusted in their hearts that he would one day come home. The years passed slowly, but the love of the city never dimmed.

At last the long wait ended. When news came that Rama was returning, the whole of Ayodhya rushed to prepare. They cleaned every path, strung garlands across every doorway, and lit rows upon rows of small clay lamps so that the entire city glowed in the night. When Rama finally arrived, the people wept with joy and welcomed him home with open arms. The longest of waits, they learned, can end in the brightest of welcomes — and this is why, to this day, we light lamps and call it Diwali.
body.hi:
Bahut samay pehle, priya rajkumar Ram ko unke rajya Ayodhya se chaudah lambe saalon ke liye door bhej diya gaya. Jangalon aur kathinaiyon ke beech, woh aur unki patni Sita aur unke vafaadar bhai Lakshman aage badhte rahe, kabhi apni himmat ya apni achhai nahi khoyi, sabse mushkil dinon mein bhi.

Ayodhya mein, log intezaar aur ummeed karte rahe. Unhone Ram ko kabhi nahi bhulaaya, aur unhe apne dil mein bharosa tha ki woh ek din ghar lautenge. Saal dheere-dheere beete, par shehar ka pyaar kabhi kam nahi hua.

Aakhir lamba intezaar khatam hua. Jab khabar aayi ki Ram laut rahe hain, poora Ayodhya taiyaari mein jut gaya. Unhone har raah saaf ki, har darwaaze par maalaayein lagaayi, aur kataar dar kataar chhote mitti ke deepak jalaaye taaki poora shehar raat mein jagmagaaye. Jab Ram aakhir pohanche, log khushi se roye aur unhe khuli baahon se ghar swagat kiya. Sabse lamba intezaar, unhone seekha, sabse roshan swagat mein khatam ho sakta hai — aur isi liye, aaj tak, hum deepak jalaate hain aur ise Diwali kehte hain.

title.en: Tell your baby the very first thing you want to say to them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko woh sabse pehli baat bataayein jo aap unse kehna chaahti hain.

motivation.en: The words you choose now are the first welcome they will ever carry.
motivation.hi: Jo shabd aap abhi chunti hain woh pehla swagat hain jo woh kabhi apne saath rakhenge.

title.hi: Har Din Banna Hai

affirmation.en: Day by day, we are both becoming who we are meant to be. There is no rush, only growing.
affirmation.hi: Din-ba-din, hum dono wahi ban rahe hain jo humein banna hai. Koi jaldi nahi, sirf badhna hai.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: When Eating Feels Like A Chore
title.hi: Jab Khaana Ek Bojh Sa Lage

content.en: Some days, the thought of preparing a meal feels like one task too many. On those days, be gentle with yourself — a handful of nuts, a piece of fruit, a warm glass of milk all count. Nourishing yourself does not have to look like a perfect plate. It only has to be kind, and small, and enough for right now.
content.hi: Kuch din, khaana banane ka khayaal ek zyada kaam jaisa lagta hai. Un dinon, khud par narm rahein — mutthi bhar nuts, ek phal, ek garm glass doodh, sab gineti mein aate hain. Khud ko poshan dena ek perfect thaali jaisa dikhna zaroori nahi. Use bas dayaalu, chhota, aur abhi ke liye kaafi hona chahiye.

remember.en: A small, kind bite is still nourishment.
remember.hi: Ek chhota, dayaalu nivaala bhi phir bhi poshan hai.

babyLearning.en: tiny muscles growing stronger, beginning to move more freely
babyLearning.hi: nanhe maaspeshiyaan mazboot ho rahi hain, zyada khulkar hilna shuru

title.en: Anticipation Is A Kind Of Love
title.hi: Intezaar Bhi Ek Tarah Ka Pyaar Hai

insight.en: The way you long for your baby, picture them, wait for them — that longing itself is love, already flowing toward them.
insight.hi: Jis tarah aap apne baby ke liye taras-ti hain, unhe sochti hain, unka intezaar karti hain — woh chaahat khud pyaar hai, jo pehle se unki taraf beh raha hai.

expanded.en: We often think love begins the moment we first hold someone. But the ache of waiting, the daydreaming about a tiny face, the way your mind drifts to them a hundred times a day — all of this is love too. Anticipation is not just impatience; it is the heart practising how to hold someone it has not yet met. Each time you wonder about your baby, you are loving them. So if the waiting feels long, let it be tender rather than hard. The longing you feel now is simply love arriving early, ahead of the one it belongs to.
expanded.hi: Hum aksar sochte hain ki pyaar us pal shuru hota hai jab hum pehli baar kisi ko thaamte hain. Par intezaar ki tadap, ek nanhe chehre ki kalpana, jis tarah aapka mann din mein sau baar unki taraf bhatakta hai — yeh sab bhi pyaar hai. Intezaar sirf besabri nahi hai; yeh dil ka abhyaas hai ki kisi aise ko kaise thaama jaaye jisse woh abhi mila nahi. Har baar jab aap apne baby ke baare mein sochti hain, aap unhe pyaar kar rahi hain. To agar intezaar lamba lage, ise kathin ke bajaaye naram rehne dein. Aap abhi jo chaahat mehsoos karti hain woh bas pyaar hai jo jaldi aa gaya, us insaan se pehle jiska woh hai.

deepDive.en: Researchers find that the bond a parent feels during pregnancy — called prenatal attachment — predicts warm, responsive caregiving after birth. The love you grow now genuinely lays the ground for later.
deepDive.hi: Researchers paate hain ki pregnancy ke dauraan ek parent jo bond mehsoos karta hai — jise prenatal attachment kehte hain — woh janm ke baad garmjoshi bhari, responsive dekhbhaal ki nishaani hai. Aap abhi jo pyaar badhaati hain woh sach mein aage ke liye neenv rakhta hai.

remember.en: Your longing for your baby is love arriving early.
remember.hi: Apne baby ke liye aapki chaahat jaldi aaya hua pyaar hai.

title.hi: Bahaadur Nanha Tota

summary.en: A small parrot's selfless courage in a forest fire moves the heavens themselves.
summary.hi: Ek nanhe tote ki nishkaam himmat jangal ki aag mein khud aasmaan ko bhi pighla deti hai.

body.en:
In a great forest lived a little parrot who loved her home and all the creatures in it. One dry summer, a fierce fire broke out and spread quickly through the trees. The frightened animals fled, and the little parrot flew to safety with them.

But she could not rest. Her heart ached for the creatures still trapped in the flames. So she flew to the river, dipped her small wings into the water, and flew back over the blaze, shaking a few precious drops down onto the fire. Again and again she flew — river to flames, flames to river — though her wings grew tired and singed.

High above, the gods watched this tiny bird trying to put out a whole forest fire with drops of water. 'It is hopeless,' some laughed. But one was so moved by her selfless courage that he wept, and his great tears fell as cool rain over the forest, quenching the fire at last. The little parrot's love, small as it seemed, had called down help from the heavens.
body.hi:
Ek bade jangal mein ek nanhi tota rehti thi jo apne ghar aur usme rehne wale sabhi jeevon se pyaar karti thi. Ek sookhi garmi mein, ek bhayankar aag lagi aur jaldi pedon mein phail gayi. Darre hue jaanwar bhaag gaye, aur nanhi tota bhi unke saath surakshit udd gayi.

Par woh chain se nahi reh saki. Uska dil un jeevon ke liye tadap raha tha jo abhi bhi aag mein phanse the. To woh nadi tak udi, apne chhote pankh paani mein dubaye, aur aag ke upar wapas udi, kuch keemti boondein aag par hilaa kar giraayi. Baar-baar woh udi — nadi se aag, aag se nadi — chaahe uske pankh thak gaye aur jal gaye.

upar ooper, devta is nanhi chidiya ko paani ki boondon se poore jangal ki aag bujhaane ki koshish karte dekh rahe the. 'Yeh bekaar hai,' kuch hanse. Par ek uski nishkaam himmat se itna pighal gaya ki woh ro pada, aur uske bade aansu jangal par thandi baarish ban kar gire, aakhir aag bujha di. Nanhi tota ka pyaar, chaahe kitna chhota laga, aasmaan se madad bula laaya tha.

title.en: Tell your baby about all the people who are already waiting to hold them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko un sabhi logon ke baare mein bataayein jo pehle se unhe thaamne ka intezaar kar rahe hain.

motivation.en: Your baby is arriving into arms that are already open and waiting.
motivation.hi: Aapka baby un baahon mein aa raha hai jo pehle se khuli aur intezaar mein hain.

title.hi: Raag Hansdhwani

description.hi: Ek khushnuma, shubh shaam ka raag

affirmation.en: As this bright melody rises, so does a joyful welcome in my heart, for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh chamkili dhun uthti hai, waise hi mere dil mein ek khushnuma swagat uthta hai, tumhare liye.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.en: The Waiting Is Part Of The Love
title.hi: Intezaar Bhi Pyaar Ka Hissa Hai

content.en: I will not rush these days away. Even the waiting is time I am spending loving my baby.
content.hi: Main in dinon ko jaldi nahi guzaarungi. Intezaar bhi woh samay hai jo main apne baby ko pyaar karte hue bita rahi hoon.

remember.en: Even the waiting days are loving days.
remember.hi: Intezaar ke din bhi pyaar ke din hain.

babyLearning.en: growing softer features, a face all its own taking shape
babyLearning.hi: narm naqsh badh rahe hain, apna ek khaas chehra aakaar le raha hai

title.hi: Safar Ke Liye Kritagyata

insight.en: Before you reach the destination, it is worth pausing to be grateful for the road that brought you here — every step of it.
insight.hi: Manzil tak pohanchne se pehle, ruk kar us raah ke liye shukrguzaar hona zaroori hai jo aapko yahan laayi — uske har kadam ke liye.

expanded.en: It is natural to keep our eyes on what is ahead — the due date, the birth, the homecoming. But the first trimester has carried its own quiet gifts: the day you first knew, the wonder you felt, the strength your body has shown, the love that arrived faster than you expected. Pausing to feel grateful for the journey so far does not mean rushing it or wishing it away. It means honouring how far you have already travelled. Gratitude turns the long road into something tender, and reminds you that this whole season — not just its ending — is a gift.
expanded.hi: Aage par nazar rakhna swabhaavik hai — due date, janm, ghar aana. Par pehle trimester ne apne shaant tohfe diye hain: woh din jab pehli baar pata chala, woh acharaj jo aapne mehsoos kiya, woh taakat jo aapke sharir ne dikhaayi, woh pyaar jo aapki ummeed se zyada jaldi aaya. Ab tak ke safar ke liye shukrguzaar hone ke liye rukna iska matlab use jaldbaazi karna ya use door chaahna nahi hai. Iska matlab hai yeh samman karna ki aap pehle se kitni door safar kar chuki hain. Kritagyata lambi raah ko kuch naram bana deti hai, aur yaad dilaati hai ki yeh poora mausam — sirf iska ant nahi — ek tohfa hai.

deepDive.en: Studies on gratitude show that regularly noticing what we are thankful for measurably lifts mood and lowers stress. A few grateful moments a day quietly reshape how we feel about everything else.
deepDive.hi: Kritagyata par hui studies dikhaati hain ki niyamit roop se yeh note karna ki hum kis cheez ke liye aabhaari hain mood ko maapne layak uthaata hai aur tanaav ghataata hai. Din ke kuch shukrguzaar pal chupchaap badal dete hain ki hum baaki har cheez ke baare mein kaisa mehsoos karte hain.

remember.en: Gratitude makes the long road tender.
remember.hi: Kritagyata lambi raah ko naram bana deti hai.

title.en: Birbal And The Most Beautiful Child
title.hi: Birbal Aur Sabse Sundar Bachcha

summary.en: Birbal answers a tricky question about beauty with a truth every parent already knows.
summary.hi: Birbal sundarta ke ek mushkil sawaal ka jawaab us sachai se deta hai jise har parent pehle se jaanta hai.

body.en:
One day in the royal court, Emperor Akbar posed a playful challenge to his wise minister Birbal. 'Birbal,' he said, 'go out into my kingdom and bring back the most beautiful child you can find. I wish to see the loveliest little one in all the land.'

Birbal bowed and set off. He travelled through villages and towns, visited grand houses and humble huts, and saw a great many children. Days later, he returned to court carrying a small, ordinary-looking baby — a child quite plain to every eye but one.

Akbar was puzzled. 'Birbal, this is hardly the most beautiful child in the kingdom!' Birbal smiled and gently handed the baby back to the woman beside him — its mother, whose face shone with joy as she took her child. 'Your Majesty,' said Birbal, 'to every mother, her own child is the most beautiful in the world. There is no other beauty quite like the one a parent sees.'
body.hi:
Ek din raajdarbaar mein, Badshah Akbar ne apne samajhdaar mantri Birbal ko ek mazaakiya chunauti di. 'Birbal,' usne kaha, 'mere rajya mein jaao aur jo sabse sundar bachcha mil sake use le aao. Main poori dharti ka sabse pyaara nanha dekhna chaahta hoon.'

Birbal jhuka aur nikal pada. Woh gaon aur shehron se guzra, bade gharon aur chhoti jhopdiyon mein gaya, aur bahut saare bachche dekhe. Kuch dinon baad, woh darbaar mein ek chhota, saadharan dikhne wala bachcha leke laut aaya — ek aisa bachcha jo ek ke siva har aankh ko bilkul saadaa lagta tha.

Akbar uljhan mein pad gaya. 'Birbal, yeh to mushkil se rajya ka sabse sundar bachcha hai!' Birbal muskuraaya aur pyaar se bachche ko apne bagal khadi aurat ko wapas thama diya — uski maa, jiska chehra apne bachche ko leke khushi se chamak utha. 'Jahaanpanah,' Birbal ne kaha, 'har maa ke liye, uska apna bachcha duniya ka sabse sundar hai. ek parent jo dekhta hai uski tarah koi doosri sundarta nahi hai.'

title.en: Tell your baby one gentle hope you hold for their very first day in the world.
title.hi: Apne baby ko ek naram ummeed bataayein jo aap duniya mein unke pehle din ke liye rakhti hain.

motivation.en: The hopes you whisper now become the welcome they are born into.
motivation.hi: Jo ummeedein aap abhi dheere se kehti hain woh woh swagat ban jaati hain jismein woh janm lete hain.

title.en: I Am Grateful For This Journey
title.hi: Main Is Safar Ke Liye Aabhaari Hoon

affirmation.en: I am thankful for every day we have shared so far, and for every one still to come.
affirmation.hi: Main har us din ke liye aabhaari hoon jo humne ab tak baanta hai, aur har us din ke liye jo abhi aana baaki hai.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.hi: Shukrana Ki Ek Saans

content.en: Breathe in slowly and bring to mind one thing you are grateful for today. Breathe out and let yourself feel it warmly. Repeat gently three times.
content.hi: Dheere se saans andar lein aur aaj jiske liye aap aabhaari hain woh ek cheez mann mein laayein. Saans baahar chhodein aur use garmjoshi se mehsoos hone dein. Pyaar se teen baar dohraayein.

remember.en: A single grateful breath can warm a whole day.
remember.hi: Ek shukrana ki saans ek poore din ko garm kar sakti hai.

babyLearning.en: growing peacefully, settled and safe within you
babyLearning.hi: shaanti se badh raha hai, aapke andar bas-a hua aur surakshit

title.hi: Jo Aage Aaye Us Par Bharosa Karein

insight.en: You cannot see the whole road ahead, and you do not need to. You only need to trust the next gentle step.
insight.hi: Aap aage ki poori raah nahi dekh sakti, aur aapko zaroorat bhi nahi. Aapko bas agle naram kadam par bharosa karna hai.

expanded.en: So much about what lies ahead is unknown — the birth, the early days, the kind of parent you will be. The mind likes to grasp for certainty, to plan every turn. But growth has never needed us to see the whole path; a seed does not know what the tree will look like, yet it grows all the same. You can hold your hopes lightly and let the future unfold at its own pace. Trusting what comes next does not mean having no fears. It means choosing, gently and again, to believe that you and your baby will meet whatever comes — together.
expanded.hi: Aage jo hai uske baare mein bahut kuch anjaan hai — janm, shuruaati din, aap kaisi parent banengi. Mann nishchitta pakadna chaahta hai, har mod plan karna. Par badhne ne kabhi humse poori raah dekhne ki maang nahi ki; ek beej nahi jaanta ki ped kaisa dikhega, phir bhi woh badhta hai. Aap apni ummeedein halke se thaam sakti hain aur bhavishya ko apni raftaar se khulne de sakti hain. Jo aage aaye us par bharosa karne ka matlab koi dar na hona nahi hai. Iska matlab hai narmi se, baar-baar, yeh maanna chunna ki aap aur aapka baby jo bhi aaye uska saamna karenge — saath.

deepDive.en: Psychologists note that tolerating uncertainty — rather than fighting it — is linked to lower anxiety and greater calm. We feel steadier not when we control the future, but when we make peace with not knowing it.
deepDive.hi: Psychologists batate hain ki anishchitta ko sehna — uske khilaaf ladne ke bajaaye — kam ghabraahat aur zyada shaanti se juda hai. Hum tab zyada sthir mehsoos karte hain jab hum bhavishya control nahi karte, balki use na jaanne ke saath shaanti banaate hain.

remember.en: You only need to trust the next gentle step.
remember.hi: Aapko bas agle naram kadam par bharosa karna hai.

title.hi: Dayaalu Kisaan Ki Fasal

summary.en: A farmer who shares his seed freely discovers that kindness returns to him in the harvest.
summary.hi: Ek kisaan jo apna beej khule dil se baantta hai paata hai ki dayaalta fasal mein use lautti hai.

body.en:
There was once a kind farmer who grew the finest corn in his whole region, winning the prize at the fair year after year. But each season, after the harvest, he did something his neighbours found very strange: he shared his best seed freely with every other farmer around him, even those whose fields bordered his own.

'Why do you give away your best seed?' a puzzled neighbour asked. 'Surely they will grow corn as good as yours and beat you at the fair.'

The farmer only smiled. 'The wind carries pollen from field to field,' he said. 'If my neighbours grow poor corn, the wind will carry their weaker pollen to my crop and spoil it. But if everyone around me grows strong, healthy corn, then my own corn grows better too. To raise the best harvest, I must help my neighbours raise theirs.' And so, year after year, his generosity quietly returned to him in his own golden fields.
body.hi:
Ek baar ek dayaalu kisaan tha jo apne poore ilaake mein sabse achha makka ugaata tha, saal dar saal mele mein inaam jeet-ta. Par har mausam, fasal ke baad, woh kuch aisa karta jo uske padosiyon ko bahut ajeeb lagta: woh apna sabse achha beej apne aas-paas ke har doosre kisaan ke saath khule dil se baant-ta, un logon ke saath bhi jinke khet uske khet se lagte the.

'Tum apna sabse achha beej kyun de dete ho?' ek uljhe padosi ne poocha. 'Woh to tumhare jaisa hi achha makka ugaayenge aur mele mein tumhe haraa denge.'

Kisaan bas muskuraaya. 'Hawa khet se khet tak paraag le jaati hai,' usne kaha. 'Agar mere padosi kamzor makka ugaayenge, hawa unka kamzor paraag meri fasal tak laayegi aur use kharaab kar degi. Par agar mere aas-paas sab mazboot, swasth makka ugaayenge, to mera apna makka bhi behtar ugega. Sabse achhi fasal uthaane ke liye, mujhe apne padosiyon ki fasal uthaane mein madad karni hogi.' Aur is tarah, saal dar saal, uski udaarta chupchaap uske apne sunhre kheton mein use lauti.

title.en: Tell your baby what you will whisper to them the very first time you hold them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap pehli baar jab unhe thaamengi tab unke kaan mein kya kahengi.

motivation.en: Saying those words now is a gentle rehearsal of a moment you already love.
motivation.hi: Woh shabd abhi kehna ek aise pal ka naram abhyaas hai jise aap pehle se pyaar karti hain.

title.hi: Bhavishya Ko Hone Dena

description.en: A meditation to soften worry about what's ahead
description.hi: Aage ki chinta ko naram karne ka ek dhyan

affirmation.en: I do not need to hold the whole future today. I only need to hold this breath, with you.
affirmation.hi: Mujhe aaj poora bhavishya thaamne ki zaroorat nahi. Mujhe bas yeh saans thaamni hai, tumhare saath.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths. As thoughts of the future arise — questions, plans, worries — gently notice them, and let each one drift past like a cloud, returning your attention to your breath and to your baby resting within you.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein. Jaise bhavishya ke vichaar aayein — sawaal, plan, chintayein — unhe pyaar se note karein, aur har ek ko ek baadal ki tarah guzar jaane dein, apna dhyan wapas apni saans par aur apne andar aaram karte baby par laate hue.

closingThought.en: Whatever comes, you will meet it one breath at a time, and never alone.
closingThought.hi: Jo bhi aaye, aap use ek-ek saans karke milengi, aur kabhi akeli nahi.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Main Jo Aaye Use Sambhaal Sakti Hoon

content.en: I do not need every answer today. I trust that I will find my way, one step at a time, when each moment arrives.
content.hi: Mujhe aaj har jawaab ki zaroorat nahi. Mujhe bharosa hai ki main apna raasta dhoondh lungi, ek-ek kadam karke, jab har pal aayega.

remember.en: You will find your way as the road appears.
remember.hi: Jaise raah saamne aayegi, aap apna raasta dhoondh lengi.

babyLearning.en: closing the first trimester, growing strong and ready
babyLearning.hi: pehla trimester poora karte hue, mazboot aur taiyaar hote hue

title.hi: Apne Samay Par Taiyaar

insight.en: Your baby will grow and arrive in their own time, not a moment too early or too late. And so will you, as a parent.
insight.hi: Aapka baby apne samay par badhega aur aayega, na ek pal jaldi na der se. Aur aap bhi, ek parent ke roop mein.

expanded.en: As the first trimester closes, it is natural to wonder if you are doing enough, growing fast enough, ready enough. But nature does not hurry, and it leaves nothing out. Your baby is unfolding at exactly the pace they are meant to, and you are becoming a parent at exactly the pace you are meant to. There is no race here, and no falling behind. Trust the slow, sure rhythm of this becoming. Everything that needs to grow is growing, quietly and on time. You do not have to rush to be ready — you are arriving, gently, just as you should.
expanded.hi: Jaise pehla trimester poora hota hai, yeh sochna swabhaavik hai ki kya aap kaafi kar rahi hain, kaafi tezi se badh rahi hain, kaafi taiyaar hain. Par prakriti jaldbaazi nahi karti, aur woh kuch nahi chhodti. Aapka baby theek us raftaar par khul raha hai jis par use khulna hai, aur aap theek us raftaar par parent ban rahi hain jis par aapko banna hai. Yahan koi daud nahi hai, aur na hi peeche rehna. Is banne ki dheemi, pakki lay par bharosa karein. Jo kuch badhna zaroori hai woh badh raha hai, chupchaap aur samay par. Taiyaar hone ke liye aapko jaldbaazi karne ki zaroorat nahi — aap aa rahi hain, narmi se, bilkul jaise aapko aana chahiye.

deepDive.en: Developmental science shows growth follows its own internal timetable that cannot be rushed — pushing harder does not speed it up. Trusting the natural pace is not passivity; it is wisdom.
deepDive.hi: Developmental science dikhaati hai ki badhna apni ek andaruni samay-saarni ka paalan karta hai jise jaldi nahi kiya ja sakta — zyada zor lagana use tez nahi karta. Prakritik raftaar par bharosa karna nishkriyata nahi hai; yeh samajhdaari hai.

remember.en: You are arriving, gently, just as you should.
remember.hi: Aap aa rahi hain, narmi se, bilkul jaise aapko aana chahiye.

title.hi: Basant Ki Pehli Kali

summary.en: A little bud learns there is no shame in blooming gently, in its own season.
summary.hi: Ek nanhi kali seekhti hai ki apne mausam mein, narmi se khilne mein koi sharm nahi.

body.en:
On a bare branch at the end of winter, a small bud sat tightly closed, waiting. All around it, the other buds began to open early, eager to be first. 'Hurry!' they called. 'Open now, before the rest! The first to bloom is the most admired.' But the little bud felt it was not yet time, and so, gently, it stayed closed and waited.

Then one night a late frost crept in. The buds that had rushed open were caught by the cold, and their petals browned and fell. But the little bud, still wrapped safe and snug, was unharmed.

When the true warmth of spring finally came, the little bud unfolded slowly, petal by petal, into a blossom more perfect than any other on the branch. The bees came first to her, and she lasted longest of all. 'You see,' whispered the old tree, 'there is no need to rush your blooming. The flower that opens in its own right time opens best of all.'
body.hi:
Sardi ke ant mein ek nangi shaakh par, ek chhoti kali kasi hui band baithi thi, intezaar karti. Uske chaaron aur, doosri kaliyaan jaldi khulne lagi, pehle hone ke liye betaab. 'Jaldi karo!' woh pukaarti. 'Abhi khulo, baaki se pehle! Pehle khilne wali sabse zyada saraahi jaati hai.' Par nanhi kali ko laga ki abhi samay nahi hai, aur is tarah, narmi se, woh band rahi aur intezaar karti rahi.

Phir ek raat ek der se aaya paala ghus aaya. Jo kaliyaan jaldi khul gayi thi woh thand mein pakdi gayi, aur unki pankhudiyaan bhoori ho kar gir gayi. Par nanhi kali, abhi bhi surakshit aur aaram se lipti, bach gayi.

Jab basant ki sachi garmi aakhir aayi, nanhi kali dheere-dheere khuli, pankhudi dar pankhudi, ek aise phool mein jo shaakh par kisi bhi aur se zyada perfect tha. Bhauren sabse pehle uske paas aaye, aur woh sabse zyada der tak rahi. 'Dekho,' boodhe ped ne dheere se kaha, 'apne khilne mein jaldbaazi karne ki zaroorat nahi. Jo phool apne sahi samay par khulta hai woh sabse achha khilta hai.'

title.en: As the first trimester closes, welcome your baby once more — tell them how glad you are they are here.
title.hi: Jaise pehla trimester poora hota hai, apne baby ka ek baar phir swagat karein — bataayein ki aap kitni khush hain ki woh yahan hain.

motivation.en: You have walked these first weeks together — let your baby hear how much that means to you.
motivation.hi: Aapne yeh pehle hafte saath chale hain — apne baby ko sunaayein ki yeh aapke liye kitna maayne rakhta hai.

title.en: We Have Come This Far Together
title.hi: Hum Yahan Tak Saath Aaye Hain

description.en: A closing affirmation for the first trimester
description.hi: Pehle trimester ke liye ek samaapan affirmation

affirmation.en: We have walked these first weeks side by side, and I will walk every week ahead with you too.
affirmation.hi: Hum yeh pehle hafte kandhe se kandha mila kar chale hain, aur main aage ka har hafta bhi tumhare saath chalungi.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: I Am Exactly Where I Need To Be
title.hi: Main Theek Wahin Hoon Jahan Mujhe Hona Chahiye

content.en: I will not measure myself against any clock or anyone else. My baby and I are growing in our own perfect time.
content.hi: Main khud ko kisi ghadi ya kisi aur se nahi naapungi. Mera baby aur main apne khud ke perfect samay mein badh rahe hain.

remember.en: Your own time is the right time.
remember.hi: Aapka apna samay hi sahi samay hai.
